channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran Has Been Consistent in War. Will It Be Consistent in Peace Talks?
item.description: While President Trump’s war aims have changed by the moment, Iran has stuck to firm demands. The question is whether it will compromise in peace negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pro-government demonstrators burning the American and Israeli flags on Wednesday in Tehran after a cease-fire was announced.

item.title: How Lu Xun, a Famous Chinese Writer, Became a Cute Communist Mascot
item.description: The Chinese Communist Party has turned Lu Xun, a Mao-era hero who excoriated the establishment, into a bland, Disney-style character.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Qilai Shen
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times

item.title: In New War With Israel, Hezbollah Defies Notion That It Was Crippled
item.description: The Iran-backed Lebanese militant group surprised many with the intensity of its attacks on Israel in the current conflict.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Israeli soldier at the site of a Hezbollah missile strike in Haniel, Israel, last month.

item.title: Growing List of Orban Loyalists Defecting Before Critical Election
item.description: With Prime Minister Viktor Orban seeming vulnerable before Sunday’s vote, criticism is growing from within institutions his party once counted on for support.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Zalan Alkonyi in Budapest, Hungary’s capital. As a researcher focused on Russia, he says he has come under pressure to toe the government line.

item.title: Iranian Delegation Arrives at Peace Talks, With Vance en Route
item.description: There was much uncertainty about the durability of the cease-fire and whether the two sides could reach a long-term deal, as Vice President JD Vance traveled to Pakistan for the talks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Lebanon: Images of a Nation Uprooted by War
item.description: A million people in Lebanon have fled their homes as Israel clashes anew with Hezbollah. This is what their lives look now.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder, Diego Ibarra Sanchez and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Two sisters from one of the many displaced Lebanese families that have set up tents near Beirut’s main sports stadium.

item.title: This Hungarian Town Explains Why Orban Could Lose on Sunday
item.description: Lake Balaton was beloved as a vacation spot. Now, luxury real estate projects serve many friends of Prime Minister Viktor Orban, and the locals are fed up.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peter Magyar, who leads the opposition Tisza party, at a rally in Keszthely last month.

item.title: Xi Meets Taiwan Opposition Leader for First Time in a Decade
item.description: Xi Jinping held rare talks with a Taiwanese opposition leader, Cheng Li-wun, who floated the idea of some day inviting Mr. Xi to visit Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: News coverage of the meeting between President Xi Jinping of China and Cheng Li-wun, the leader of the Nationalist Party in Taiwan, showing in a restaurant in Taipei, on Friday.

item.title: Prince Harry Is Sued for Libel by a Charity He Founded
item.description: Sentebale filed a defamation lawsuit against the Duke of Sussex, who resigned as patron last year after a clash between the board of trustees and the board’s chair.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Prince Harry in a confidence building exercise with young people at the Kasane Health Post in Botswana, during a summit run by Sentebale in 2019.

item.title: ‘I’m Fed Up.’ Frustrated With Trump, Starmer Embraces Other Allies.
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain is strengthening ties in Europe and the Middle East as the once-special relationship with America sours.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain boarding an airplane, in Doha, Qatar, on Friday, after a three-day trip to the Gulf.

item.title: Should Churches Remove Art by a Priest Accused of Sexual Abuse?
item.description: The Rev. Marko Ivan Rupnik has installed mosaics across the Roman Catholic world, including at the Vatican. After nuns accused him of abuse, some want his work removed.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:description: Covering a mosaic made by the Rev. Marko Ivan Rupnik at Notre-Dame-du-Rosaire Basilica in Lourdes, France, in 2025.

item.title: Iran’s South Africa Mission Trolls Trump Amid Hormuz Ceasefire
item.description: Official social media accounts for Iranian embassies have engaged in information warfare, but the ones in Africa appear to have taken the lead on the attacks.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranian pro-government demonstrators rally in Tehran on Wednesday after the announcement of a two-week cease-fire in the war against the United States and Israel.

item.title: Many Venezuelans Want to Go Home. But They Can’t Get Passports.
item.description: The U.S. has made it impossible for them to stay legally, and their own country makes it difficult for them to return.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Sulbarán
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Yoalbert González has bought a ticket to return to Venezuela, but the American government still has his passport.

item.title: With Iran Setting Limits, Strait of Hormuz Remains Thorny Politically
item.description: Even after a cease-fire, Iran is keeping a chokehold on traffic, forcing countries to cut deals that could put them at odds with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard that appeared over the weekend in Tehran shows Iranian soldiers with American military planes and ships caught in a net, with the message “The Strait of Hormuz will stay closed.”

item.title: See You in Pyongyang: Russia Pushes Its People to Embrace North Korea
item.description: Through art, food, tourism and academics, an increasingly isolated Moscow is seeking to cement lasting ties with Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Nanna Heitmann
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: A student sharing his experiences from a recent trip to North Korea, at a lecture Moscow State University’s Institute of Asian Studies, in Moscow in December.

item.title: South African Writer Mfundi Vundla Gets Personal in His New Play
item.description: Mfundi Vundla spent 21 years in exile and created the popular television show “Generations.” His latest project is a play that explores the imperfections of the fight against apartheid.
item.dc:creator: Zimasa Matiwane
item.media:description: Mfundi Vundla, South African activist and writer, at the Johannesburg Theater after a performance of his new play, “Man With No Surname,” in March.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Tyler Pager and John Yoon

item.title: Iran Looks to Project Unity With Large Delegation to Peace Talks
item.description: At least 70 people are in the team that is scheduled to negotiate with the American side in Pakistan on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pakistan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, via Reuters
item.media:description: A photograph released by Pakistan’s government showing the Iranian delegation led by the speaker of Iran’s parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, second from right; and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, second from left. They were welcomed by Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, left; and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, right, in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

item.title: Fallout of War Piles Economic Pain Onto Europe’s Political Stress
item.description: Europe is finding itself on the outs with Russia, China and the U.S., in what’s amounting to its very own “Mean Girls” moment.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Patrick Junker for The New York Times
item.media:description: A liquefied natural gas terminal in Germany. Most of that L.N.G. comes from the United States, underscoring Europe’s vulnerability to American supplies.

item.title: World Leaders Push to Save Iran Talks Amid Israel’s Attacks in Lebanon
item.description: President Trump’s cease-fire with Iran appears at risk as Vice President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for discussions with Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A Lebanese man stood on a pile of debris in the Corniche El Mazraa area of Beirut after the Israeli bombardment of the capital on Wednesday.

item.title: What the Cease-Fire Means for Iran
item.description: Emerging from weeks of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes, an emboldened Iran has 10 demands for talks during the tenuous cease-fire, according to Iranian state media. Our reporter Erika Solomon assesses Iran’s position.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Christina Thornell, David Seekamp and Joey Sendaydiego

item.title: University of Ottawa Lockdown Lifted After a Man Is Arrested
item.description: The university locked down the campus for just under two hours on Friday after reports that a man was acting suspiciously nearby. Police would not say if he was armed.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: The University of Ottawa in Canada’s capital on Friday.

item.title: New Evidence Further Implicates U.S. Missiles in Strikes That Killed 21 Civilians in Iran
item.description: Additional images and video build on an earlier analysis, which the Pentagon has disputed, showing Precision Strike Missiles, or PrSMs, hit a sports hall and residential areas in the Iranian city of Lamerd.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert, John Ismay, Alexander Cardia, Leanne Abraham and Azmat Khan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Trump Is Racing to Redefine ‘America First’ in a Time of War
item.description: Washington has been debating the thrust of Mr. Trump’s foreign policy for more than a decade, with the only consensus being that “America First” means whatever the president says it does.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump holding a “Make America Great Again” hat on his way to his golf club in Miami last year.

item.title: With U.S.-Iran Peace Talks Set to Begin, Prospects for Long-Term Deal Uncertain
item.description: With U.S. and Iranian leaders heading to Pakistan for negotiations amid a two-week cease-fire, the two sides must resolve major differences on Lebanon, the Strait of Hormuz and frozen Iranian assets.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Adam Rasgon, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Before his departure on Friday for talks in Pakistan, Vice President JD Vance said he believed the negotiations were “going to be positive,” but he also had a warning for Iran.

item.title: Facing Many Crises, Pakistan Tries to End a Big One — in Iran
item.description: A nation with a stalled economy, a terrorism problem and two hostile neighbors is set to host the first formal U.S.-Iranian talks since the war began.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Watching coverage of the Iran war at a barbershop in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

item.title: Why Opening the Strait of Hormuz Won’t Immediately Lower Gas Prices
item.description: Our energy reporter Rebecca F. Elliott explains why, even if the flow of energy is restored through the Persian Gulf, it will take months to carry out repairs across dozens of energy sites in the region.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott, Coleman Lowndes, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Stephanie Swart, Nikolay Nikolov and Thomas Vollkommer

item.title: Only two ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.media:description: A tanker moored in Muscat, Oman, last month. Hundreds of ships are waiting on either side of the Strait of Hormuz as Iran and the United States seek to negotiate an end to the war.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Plan Talks in D.C. as War Threatens U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Deal
item.description: Whether Israel’s conflict with Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group in Lebanon, is included in the Iran cease-fire is one of the major disputes rattling the truce.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Adam Rasgon, Natan Odenheimer and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Residents lifting debris from a damaged car in Beirut, Lebanon, in a neighborhood damaged by Israeli airstrikes earlier this week.

item.title: Iran Demands Release of ‘Blocked Assets’ Before Peace Talks
item.description: In what appeared to be a new condition, Iran’s parliament speaker said that negotiations set for Saturday in Pakistan would not go ahead unless unspecified Iranian funds were released.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Leily Nikounazar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, the speaker of Iran’s Parliament, has introduced conditions that could derail talks with U.S. negotiators set for this weekend.

item.title: Fuel Protests Cause Transport Chaos in Ireland as Iran War Spikes Prices
item.description: The Irish government said it had called in the army after protesters blocked highways, ports and an oil refinery, causing widespread disruption.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tractors blocking O’Connell Street in Dublin, a main artery of the capital, during a protest on Wednesday.

item.title: Why Investing in Wind and Solar to Avoid Gas Shocks Hasn’t Added Up for Some
item.description: Renewable energy is cheaper to run than fossil fuels, especially with war choking oil supply. But it hasn’t turned out that way for some European countries, and the reason is complex.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown and Mira Rojanasakul
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Islamabad, Pakistan’s quiet capital, steps into the diplomatic spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.media:description: A worker painting a street side curb near the Serena Hotel in Islamabad on Thursday.

item.title: Vance Faces Test of His Negotiating Skills With Iran Talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is leading negotiations this weekend toward an end to a war that he had opposed starting.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Elizabeth Frantz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance is traveling to Pakistan at the end of the week to hold talks with the Iranians.

item.title: Soaring Fuel Prices Squeeze China’s Frugal Truckers
item.description: Many long-haul truckers in China have a tight budget and live frugally on the road, sleeping and cooking in their vehicles. Now, the pain at the pump is forcing some drivers to rethink their lives.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang and Yasu Tsuji

item.title: Middle East War Triggers Higher Prices in China
item.description: Three and a half years of deflationary pressure on Chinese factories reversed course last month as higher energy prices cycled into the economy.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: A car factory in Chongqing, China. Falling wholesale prices have forced thousands of manufacturers to sell their goods for less and less.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Yeganeh Torbati and Adam Rasgon

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Israeli strikes against Hezbollah in Lebanon threatened the cease-fire between the United States and Iran ahead of scheduled peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Katie Reilly
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of an airstrike in the Tallet el-Khayat area of Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday.

item.title: What We Know About the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Talks in Pakistan
item.description: Vice President JD Vance will lead the United States delegation which is set to meet with Iranian officials in Pakistan on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Foreign Ministry offices in Islamabad, Pakistan. Pakistan is set to host talks between the United States and Iran on Saturday.

item.title: A Mexican Cartel Leader and Ex-Partner to El Mencho Pleads Guilty in U.S.
item.description: Erick Valencia Salazar, a co-founder of one of Mexico’s deadliest gangs, faces 10 years to life in prison after making a plea deal in a Washington court.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: Erick Valencia-Salazar, known as El 85, in Mexico City in 2012.

item.title: Amid Trump’s Threats, NATO Labors to Survive the Iran War
item.description: President Trump is citing the unwillingness of European nations to back the United States in the conflict as another reason to scale back or abandon the alliance. And he still wants Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump and Mark Rutte, the secretary general of NATO, in the Netherlands in June. The two met again in Washington this week, as the war in Iran has deepened the gulf between Mr. Trump and the alliance.

item.title: Putin Announces Orthodox Easter Cease-Fire, but Ukraine Is Skeptical
item.description: The cease-fire would be in effect this weekend, but each side accused the other of violating a similar pause announced last year.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Constant Méheut
item.media:description: The besieged city of Kostiantynivka, Ukraine, in January.

item.title: Venezuela Approves New Law to Open Mining to Foreign Investors
item.description: The move opens the country’s coveted mineral fortune up to foreign investors, the latest move that Venezuela’s leadership has taken to satisfy the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: An illegal gold mine in Las Claritas, Venezuela, in 2016.

item.title: How Iranians Feel Now
item.description: The fragile cease-fire has brought relief. But hope for change seems farther away than ever.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Zelensky Sees Small Window for Peace
item.description: Talks to end the war in Ukraine could resume soon, said President Volodymyr Zelensky as he expressed skepticism about a breakthrough.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in the city of Slovyansk, Ukraine, last month.

item.title: Netanyahu Says He Ordered Israel to Hold Talks With Lebanon About Disarming Hezbollah
item.description: But the Israeli prime minister said his country would continue its attacks against the Iran-backed armed group.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Destruction in Beirut on Thursday, a day after Israel launched an intense wave of attacks on Lebanon.

item.title: Israel Says It Will Talk With Lebanon but Gives No Sign of a Cease-Fire There
item.description: Europeans and Iran warned that the Israeli offensive against Hezbollah threatened the truce in the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Amelia Nierenberg, Christina Goldbaum and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damage on Thursday in Beirut, which the Israeli military continued to bombard.

item.title: 137-Year-Old Piece of Eiffel Tower to Be Auctioned in Paris
item.description: A section of the landmark’s original staircase will go up for auction next month and could sell for a towering sum.
item.dc:creator: Ana Castelain
item.media:description: Gustave Eiffel, left, and a friend on the spiral staircase of the newly built Eiffel Tower in 1889.

item.title: A Newspaper Is Raided and a Rights Group Outlawed Amid Kremlin Crackdown
item.description: As Moscow throttles Russia’s few remaining independent voices, the authorities targeted two of the most prominent, one a Nobel Peace Prize winner and the other headed by a Nobel laureate.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dmitry Muratov, a Nobel laureate, during a meeting at Novaya Gazeta’s office in Moscow last year.

item.title: Trump Officials Push Allies to Pursue Antifa and Far Left as Terrorist Threats
item.description: The Trump administration aims to deploy counterterrorism tools against far-left groups, even as it has offered little evidence they present a dire threat.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas, Alan Feuer, Matina Stevis-Gridneff, Edward Wong and Jim Tankersley
item.media:description: Left-wing protesters clashing with police in Milan in February. A State Department official said the episode showed why Western governments must combat “antifa and far-left terrorism.”

item.title: I.M.F. Says Iran War Will Drag Global Growth Lower
item.description: Kristalina Georgieva, the head of the International Monetary Fund, said the war in the Middle East could lead to another bout of inflation and higher interest rates.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Buildings of Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran damaged by airstrikes last week. The International Monetary Fund said on Thursday that the war would cause slower growth in the world economy this year.

item.title: Amid U.S.-Iran Negotiations, Netanyahu’s Interests May Conflict With Trump’s
item.description: As the focus shifts to negotiations between the United States and Iran, no formal Israeli participation is planned.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: At an underground bomb shelter being used as a hospital in Haifa, Israel, on Thursday, a television showed Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu addressing the nation.

item.title: 3 Russian Submarines Detected Near Britain Were Spying, U.K. Says
item.description: John Healey, the defense secretary, said the vessels were gathering information about undersea pipelines, and said he believed President Vladimir V. Putin “would want us to be distracted by the Middle East.”
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Defense Secretary John Healey said on Thursday that the U.K. military operation “reinforces the seriousness with which we take the Russian threat.”

item.title: Iran’s Battered Leaders Emerge From War Confident — and With New Cards
item.description: For Iran’s theocratic rulers, just surviving the U.S.-Israeli onslaught means victory. But the seeds of their next crisis may already be planted.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman waving an Iranian flag in Tehran. Iran’s leadership is entering into renewed negotiations after the cease-fire with a new set of demands.

item.title: European Leaders Demand That U.S. Cease-Fire With Iran Include Lebanon
item.description: Britain, France and the European Union condemned Israel’s strikes targeting Hezbollah in the country, saying these threatened the truce.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bodies in a hospital morgue after Israel launched a deadly wave of attacks in Lebanon that killed more than 200 people on Wednesday.

item.title: What to Know About Israel’s Strikes on Lebanon
item.description: Israel says the truce with Iran does not cover Lebanon. But Tehran says it does, and it has threatened to pull out of peace talks unless the bombing stops.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rescue worker on Thursday at one of the sites in Beirut, Lebanon, that Israel bombed.

item.title: North Korea Tests New Weapons, Drawing Lessons from Iran War
item.description: The country has been leveraging armed conflicts abroad, such as Russia’s war against Ukraine, to bolster its own military capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: File footage of a North Korea missile launch shown at a train station in Seoul on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Again Hits Out at NATO Over Iran War
item.description: President Trump lashed out after hosting Mark Rutte, NATO’s secretary general, at the White House on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger and Christopher F. Schuetze
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Maker of Pet Toys in Ukraine Turns to Killer Drones
item.description: An entrepreneur behind drones that make the final strike themselves epitomizes the transformation of Ukraine’s civilian technology industry into a defense powerhouse.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Mauricio Lima for The New York Times
item.media:description: A drone with a weighted dummy explosive attached.

item.title: In Germany’s East, the Far Right Could Soon Take Power. This Is Its Plan.
item.description: In the eastern state of Saxony-Anhalt, the Alternative for Germany could win control of the government this fall. Once in power, it has a plan to overhaul German society.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Christopher F. Schuetze
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supporters of the Alternative for Germany party at its election campaign launch in Halle, Germany, in January of last year.
